01a_quotes.title: The Impact of Generative Artificial Intelligence on Market Equilibrium: Evidence from a Natural Experiment

study_design: To overcome the challenge of causal inference, we identify an unanticipated and sudden leak of a highly proficient image-generative AI as a natural experiment, where markets inadvertently encounter experimental and control conditions, resembling a scenario wherein nature orchestrates an experiment.
Using a difference-in-differences research design, we compare the anime market to the wallpaper market, before and after the unintended leak of NovelAI's anime generative AI.
We employ the standard multi-periods model specification of difference-in-differences with two-way fixed effects

This leak served as an ideal natural experiment. First, China constitutes an environment where painting markets remained unaffected by generative AI prior to the leak due to policy restrictions, language restrictions, and payment restrictions. Second, the leaked AI specializes exclusively in generating anime-style images, thus affecting only the anime painting markets while leaving other painting styles unaffected, allowing for a comparison between treated and control units. Third, the unanticipated and sudden nature of the leak is exogenous.
task: Consumers, as demanders, can post an order on the focal platform, and artists, as producers, can express their interest in accepting the order. The consumer can then select one of the interested artists and enter into a contract with the selected artist. Once the artist has completed the task, the consumer makes the payment.

An order on the focal platform is comprised of an art category, a price range, and a description. The art category includes "original character", "artwork", "avatar", "Q style", "tachie", "wallpaper", "live2d", "scenario", "cover", "poster", "UI", "character design", and "CG".
population: We collected 197,068 records from a leading art outsourcing platform in China from January 2022 to February 2023.
We removed 408 outlier orders whose price is more than 70,000 yuan (about $10,000) because they are too expensive to become real orders.
We then aggregate the data to the category-week level.
arms: We select the category "tachie" as the treated market. The "tachie" is a Japanese term in the context of anime, manga, and video games that refers to the full-body standing drawing of a character. The "tachie" is precisely the style that NovelAI's AI produces.
In contrast, we select the "wallpaper" as the control market.

The orders on the focal platform are classified into two categories based on licenses: personal orders and commercial orders. The artwork of personal orders is for personal use only, whereas the artwork of commercial orders is permitted for commercial purposes.
outcome_definitions: For each category and each week, we calculate the average price (price), order volume (volume), and overall revenue (revenue).
We calculate the average price and overall revenue based on the lower bound because the lower bound may better reflect the true willingness of consumers to accept their orders than the upper bound.
Price refers to the average price. Volume refers to order volume. Revenue refers to the overall revenue. The currency of Price and Revenue is Chinese yuan (7 yuan = $1).
We define incumbents as the group of artists who registered prior to the leak, i.e., before October 2022.
statistical_results: Our analysis reveals a promising effect on market equilibrium following the emergence of generative AI. Specifically, although this emergence leads to a 64% reduction in the average price, the generative AI also leads to a remarkable 121% increase in order volume and a consequent 56% increase in overall revenue. All effects are statistically significant at the 1% level.
The coefficient estimate of price is -646, indicating generative AI lowered the average price by 646 yuan.
The coefficient estimates of volume and revenue are 140 and 56,674, showing that generative AI increased the number of orders by 140 per week and increased the total revenue by 56,674 yuan per week.
The results show that price decreased by 64%, volume increased by 121%, and revenue increased by 56%. All effects are statistically significant at the 1% level.
According to our data, the incumbents won 97.41% of the orders and 97.96% of the revenue after the leak.

Consumers can fulfill their orders 64% cheaper than before, indicating an improvement in consumer welfare. Furthermore, artists receive 56% more revenue, as each monetary transaction completed by consumers is considered revenue for the artist. They receive 121% more orders, which underscores the increased opportunities that accelerate the evolution of their artistic endeavors by providing an invaluable platform for rapid prototyping and iterative refinement.
The results in Panel B are consistent with our main effects, indicating that incumbents benefit from generative AI by gaining more orders (116%) and more revenue (65%).
ambiguity_or_caveats: However, the equation does not hold in the context of this study. A plausible explanation for this discrepancy could be the influence of seasonal effects. For example, the demand for anime images could naturally fluctuate after October 2022 due to the start of a new academic semester for students. This intrinsic variability could potentially skew our estimates of the impact of generative AI, leading to underestimates, overestimates, or even fundamentally incorrect estimates.

However, randomized experiments are not feasible in this study. While the individual level of analysis allows for the division of subjects into treatment and control groups, the challenge arises at the market level of analysis. Unlike individuals, markets cannot be randomly assigned to different groups, which limits the ability to implement generative AI effects only on the treated group while leaving the control group unaffected.

With regard to the remaining categories, they are likely to be partially affected by NovelAI's generative AI, because the generative AI may only be useful with careful prompt engineering and post-processing, which could result in an underestimation of the effects.
The "wallpaper" may be the best control unit because it is unlikely that NovelAI's generative AI will generate elaborate wallpapers.

Stable diffusion may struggle with maintaining fidelity to specific details in the text prompts, especially for complex or highly detailed descriptions, resulting in images that are somewhat generic or miss nuanced aspects of the desired output. This limitation can qualitatively explain why generative AI mainly affects low-end orders and cannot replace incumbent artists.

One potential explanation for this result is that the current generative AI is unable to fulfill orders without human artists. This is because the generation of content is an iterative process between consumers and artists, with artists required to make ongoing modifications in response to consumer feedback. The current generative AI, which outputs images, audio, and videos, lacks the capacity to make slight modifications independently due to the limitations of its underlying algorithms.

Furthermore, generative AI may present potential copyright issues that limit its commercial applications. This is because the training of generative AI often involves the use of a substantial number of images collected from the Internet without copyright permission.

01a_quotes.notes: This is an empirical study using a natural experiment (the NovelAI model leak in October 2022) to estimate causal effects of generative AI on market equilibrium. The paper uses difference-in-differences methodology comparing the anime art market (treated) to the wallpaper market (control) before and after the leak. Data comes from a Chinese art outsourcing platform (Mihuashi) covering January 2022 to February 2023. The study includes heterogeneous analyses by order type (personal vs. commercial) and supplier type (incumbents vs. entrants).

01_extraction.title: The Impact of Generative Artificial Intelligence on Market Equilibrium: Evidence from a Natural Experiment
01_extraction.study_design_description: Using a difference-in-differences research design with two-way fixed effects, the study compares the anime market (treated) to the wallpaper market (control) before and after the unanticipated leak of NovelAI's anime generative AI in October 2022. The leak served as a natural experiment because China's painting markets were previously unaffected by generative AI due to policy, language, and payment restrictions; the leaked AI specialized exclusively in anime-style images; and the leak was exogenous and sudden.
01_extraction.primary_task_description: Consumers post orders on an art outsourcing platform specifying an art category, price range, and description. Artists express interest in accepting orders, and consumers select an artist and enter into a contract. Once the artist completes the task, the consumer makes payment. Art categories include "original character", "artwork", "avatar", "Q style", "tachie", "wallpaper", "live2d", "scenario", "cover", "poster", "UI", "character design", and "CG".
01_extraction.population_description: 197,068 records were collected from a leading art outsourcing platform in China from January 2022 to February 2023. After removing 408 outlier orders priced above 70,000 yuan, data were aggregated to the category-week level. Orders were classified into personal orders (for personal use only) and commercial orders (permitted for commercial purposes).

arms.description: The "tachie" category, a Japanese term referring to full-body standing drawings of characters in anime, manga, and video games. This is precisely the style that NovelAI's AI produces.
The "wallpaper" category, selected as the control market because it is unlikely that NovelAI's generative AI will generate elaborate wallpapers.

outcomes.outcome_description: The average price of orders in each category-week. Calculated based on the lower bound of the price range because the lower bound may better reflect the true willingness of consumers to accept their orders than the upper bound. Currency is Chinese yuan (7 yuan = $1).
The order volume for each category-week, representing the number of orders.
The overall revenue for each category-week, calculated as the total monetary value of completed transactions. Currency is Chinese yuan (7 yuan = $1).

01_extraction.notes: This is a natural experiment study using the October 2022 leak of NovelAI's anime generative AI model as an exogenous shock. The treated unit is the "tachie" (anime character standing art) category, and the control is the "wallpaper" category. Data come from a Chinese art outsourcing platform covering January 2022 to February 2023 (197,068 records, 408 outliers removed). The study employs difference-in-differences with two-way fixed effects. All main effects are reported as statistically significant at the 1% level (p < 0.01). Heterogeneous analyses are provided by order type (personal vs. commercial) and supplier type (incumbents registered before October 2022 vs. entrants). The paper notes potential caveats: seasonal effects may confound estimates; randomized experiments are infeasible at the market level; other art categories may be partially affected by the AI; and generative AI may struggle with complex, detailed descriptions and lacks capacity for iterative modifications in response to feedback. Incumbents captured 97.41% of orders and 97.96% of revenue after the leak.

02_method.rationale: The study explicitly uses a "difference-in-differences research design" and describes the NovelAI leak as "a natural experiment" that was "exogenous and sudden," comparing treated (anime) vs. control (wallpaper) markets before and after the leak.

per_outcome.operationalization: Average lower bound of price range for orders in each category-week, measured in Chinese yuan
Count of orders per category-week on the platform
Total monetary value of completed transactions per category-week, measured in Chinese yuan

04_onet.rationale: The participants are artists on an outsourcing platform who create original artwork across multiple categories (character design, avatars, posters, UI, etc.). The primary task description explicitly states artists 'complete the task' of creating commissioned art based on consumer specifications. This is fundamentally creative work requiring original artistic production, which maps directly to 'Thinking Creatively' — developing and executing original ideas in visual/artistic domains.

alternates.onet_label: Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment

llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: This is a natural experiment studying the impact of NovelAI's generative AI leak on the tachie (character drawing) market. The 'treated_tachie' arm represents the market exposed to AI competition (human artists competing with/against AI), while 'control_wallpaper' represents the unexposed market. The primary comparison is the difference-in-differences effect of the AI leak.
llm_extracted.notes: This is a difference-in-differences natural experiment studying the effect of NovelAI's generative AI leak on the tachie (character art) market vs. wallpaper market. The regression coefficient represents the DiD estimator (treated×after interaction) in levels (orders per week), not log-transformed. The paper reports both the absolute effect (140 orders/week increase) and relative effect (121% increase). Without a baseline mean for the pre-treatment tachie market, we cannot directly convert the level-regression coefficient to a log ratio. The extremely small SE (4.9×10^-13) suggests possible numerical precision issues in the reported table. Sample sizes (n) are not provided for the category-week panel data structure.
source_quotes: The coefficient estimates of volume and revenue are 140 and 56,674, showing that generative AI increased the number of orders by 140 per week and increased the total revenue by 56,674 yuan per week. the generative AI also leads to a remarkable 121% increase in order volume

computed.notes: This is a difference-in-differences natural experiment studying the effect of NovelAI's generative AI leak on the tachie (character art) market vs. wallpaper market. The regression coefficient represents the DiD estimator (treated×after interaction) in levels (orders per week), not log-transformed. The paper reports both the absolute effect (140 orders/week increase) and relative effect (121% increase). Without a baseline mean for the pre-treatment tachie market, we cannot directly convert the level-regression coefficient to a log ratio. The extremely small SE (4.9×10^-13) suggests possible numerical precision issues in the reported table. Sample sizes (n) are not provided for the category-week panel data structure. | CI missing; variance not computable
